name: remove_container_preserves_owned_threads_overlap
code:
proof fn phi_1_remove_container_preserves_owned_threads_overlap(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr),
        new_container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        new_container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        new_container_perms[c1].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
        new_container_perms[c2].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: remove_container_ensures does not constrain owned_threads disjointness in new_container_perms — after removal, two containers can still claim the same thread

name: remove_container_no_resource_cleanup
code:
proof fn phi_2_remove_container_no_resource_cleanup(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr),
        old_container_perms[container_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        !new_container_perms.dom().contains(container_ptr),
{
}
reason: The removed container's owned_threads are silently dropped — remove_container_ensures removes the container from dom but never transfers or validates that owned resources (threads, endpoints, procs) are handled

name: sll_len_without_wf_returns_value_list_len
reason: StaticLinkedList::len's external_body unconditionally ensures l == self.value_list_len without requiring wf() — the raw field is exposed for malformed lists

name: unique_implys_no_duplicates_external_body
reason: unique_implys_no_duplicates is external_body — it asserts that unique() (defined over spec_seq and value_list_len) implies Seq::no_duplicates() without proof, creating a trusted bridge between two different representations

name: root_container_parent_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_5_root_container_parent_unconstrained(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(root_container),
        container_perms[root_container].value().parent.is_Some(),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: container_root_wf constrains depth==0 and non-root must have parent.is_Some(), but never asserts root's parent.is_None() — root can have a spurious parent pointer
